activities_projects.title: Build a Precision Sundial
activities_projects.objective: Design and calibrate a sundial to measure time accurately
steps: Place the stick vertically on the board
Use a compass to align the sundial with the north direction
Mark shadow positions every hour for one full day
Label time markings based on observations
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how Earth's rotation can be used to measure time
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of calibration and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Moon Phase Cycle Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Investigate the full lunar cycle and analyze patterns
steps: Observe the Moon every night for one lunar month
Record the visible shape of the Moon each day
Identify major phases such as new moon, quarter, and full moon
Analyze the pattern and duration of the cycle
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the lunar cycle and its connection to calendars
activities_projects.evaluation: Completeness of data and analysis (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Star Movement Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Investigate apparent star movement due to Earth's rotation
steps: Observe a constellation at the same time over several nights
Record its position relative to a fixed landmark
Draw the changing position of stars
Explain why the position appears to change
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands apparent star motion caused by Earth's rotation
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of observation and explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Earth–Moon–Sun Model Simulation
activities_projects.objective: Demonstrate how lunar phases and eclipses occur
steps: Use the torch as the Sun
Use the globe as Earth and the ball as Moon
Rotate the Earth and move the Moon around it
Observe phases and eclipse alignments
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Visualizes the relationship between Earth, Moon, and Sun
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity of explanation and model demonstration (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Shadow Length Time Analysis
activities_projects.objective: Analyze how shadow length changes with time of day
steps: Place a vertical stick on the ground
Measure the shadow length every hour
Record the values in a table
Plot a graph of time vs shadow length
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how the Sun’s position affects shadows and time measurement
activities_projects.evaluation: Accuracy of graph and interpretation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Constellation Tracking Project
steps: Observe a particular constellation for several weeks
Record its position and visibility
Compare observations across different nights
Explain seasonal changes in star visibility
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands Earth's movement around the Sun and seasonal star changes

activities_projects.title: Calendar Design Based on Lunar Cycle
activities_projects.objective: Design a simple calendar using lunar phases
steps: Observe the lunar cycle for one month
Record the dates of major moon phases
Design a calendar based on these phases
Compare with an existing lunar calendar
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands how lunar observations influence calendar systems

activities_projects.title: Night Sky Mapping
activities_projects.objective: Create a map of visible stars and constellations
steps: Observe the sky on a clear night
Draw the visible star patterns
Label known constellations
Compare with a star chart for accuracy

activities_projects.title: Eclipse Investigation Project
steps: Research the causes of solar and lunar eclipses
Draw diagrams explaining both phenomena
Explain the alignment of Earth, Moon, and Sun
Present the findings
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Understands the scientific cause of eclipses
activities_projects.evaluation: Clarity and correctness of explanation (10 marks)

activities_projects.title: Astronomical Timekeeping Investigation
activities_projects.objective: Explore historical methods of measuring time using the sky
steps: Research ancient timekeeping methods like sundials and lunar calendars
Identify how civilizations used the sky to measure time
Prepare a short report
Discuss how modern clocks differ
activities_projects.learning_outcome: Connects astronomy with historical timekeeping methods
activities_projects.evaluation: Depth of research and explanation (10 marks)
